title: greeter.py — guided walkthrough
subtitle: A tiny example tour. Click any line badge to jump to that line in your editor.
intro: `greeter.py` is a two-function module: it builds a greeting and prints it. This example shows the shape of a tour — replace the file path in `files`, the line numbers, and the prose with your own.

diagrams.title: Module at a glance
diagrams.code:
graph TD
  A[main argv] --> B[build_greeting]
  B --> C[greeting string]
  C --> D[print]

designNotes: **One responsibility per function.** `build_greeting` is pure (easy to test); `main` does the I/O.
**No globals.** Everything flows through arguments and return values.

sections.title: Imports
sections.body: Standard library only — `sys` for `argv`. Keeping the dependency surface small means no install step.

sections.title: build_greeting(name)
sections.body: Returns the greeting string for `name`, defaulting to `"world"` when empty. The default is applied at [[6]] so callers never have to pass one.

callouts.text: Pure function: no printing, no globals — trivially unit-testable.

sections.title: main(argv)
sections.body: Reads the first CLI argument (if any) and prints the greeting. The argument parsing is at [[13]] and the single side effect — `print` — is at [[15]].

callouts.text: `argv[1]` is guarded by a length check; indexing it directly would crash on no args.

groups.title: Module setup
groups.description: What the module pulls in before any logic runs.

groups.title: Greeting logic
groups.description: The pure core that turns a name into a greeting string.

groups.title: Command-line entry
groups.description: The I/O shell that wires the core up to `argv` and `print`.

dataFlow: **main** reads `argv` → calls **build_greeting** → prints the result. The pure core (**build_greeting**) is isolated from the I/O shell (**main**).
